claim: The Cloudflare deployment workflow now binds the exact deployment-proof bytes to the source commit, workflow builder identity, and invocation, then applies a deny-by-default trusted-builder policy before retaining the attestation artifact.

coverage: one-byte proof mutation changes SHA-256 subject digest
verification.environment_note: GitHub clone was unavailable because the execution container could not resolve github.com; tests ran in an isolated Node runtime using byte-identical committed module and test contents.

trust_limits: The attestation is not cryptographically signed.
Policy acceptance does not prove the deployment claim is true.
No Cloudflare hostname or served bytes were verified in this run.
The workflow still requires configured Cloudflare credentials.
falsification_routes: Modify one byte of cloudflare-deployment-proof.json and confirm the attested subject digest changes.
Change the builder identity or source repository and confirm deny-by-default policy rejection.
Dispatch the workflow after credentials are configured and confirm both proof and attestation artifacts are retained for the same source commit.
